events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes unsealed duct joints with cloth duct tape instead of mastic or foil tape. This is a clear non-compliance issue per IMC 603 and SMACNA standards. Duct joints must be properly sealed with approved sealants (mastic) or approved tapes (foil tape, not cloth duct tape). Cloth duct tape is explicitly not approved for duct sealing.

Decision: fail
Risk: medium (energy loss, potential air leakage, but not immediately dangerous)
Work stage: rough-complete
Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant
Percent complete: maybe 85% (roughed in but sealing not done)
Confidence: high, 0.95
Findings: unsealed duct joints, cloth duct tape used instead of approved sealant
Actions: remove cloth duct tape, seal all joints with mastic or UL-listed foil tape
References: IMC 603, SMACNA HVAC Duct Construction Standards, Manual D

  "rationale": "IMC 603 and SMACNA require duct joints and seams to be sealed with approved sealants. Cloth duct tape is not an approved sealing method and is a well-known failure point. At rough-complete stage, unsealed joints are a non-compliant defect that must be corrected before close-out.",
